title: Delicate Mexican Chicken Tlayudas
description: A malty, silky, light Mexican dish featuring chicken stock concentrate, served in a terracotta dish, paleo-friendly and perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Roast the poblano peppers in the oven for 30 minutes, then peel and dice them.
In a large pot, bring 4 cups of water to a boil, then add the chicken breast and cook for 15 minutes, or until cooked through.
Remove the chicken from the pot and immediately submerge it in an ice bath to stop the cooking process.
Once the chicken has cooled, shred it into bite-sized pieces and set aside.
In a large skillet, heat 1 tablespoon of oil over medium heat, then add the diced onion and cook until translucent.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for an additional 1 minute.
Add the shredded chicken, roasted poblano peppers, chicken stock concentrate, lime juice, salt, and pepper to the skillet, then stir to combine.
Warm the corn tortillas by wrapping them in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20 seconds.
Assemble the tlayudas by spooning the chicken mixture onto a tortilla, then serving in a terracotta dish.
